name: UBA : Subject_CN and Username Map Update
notes: This rule detects when a VPN event's Username is not equal to 'VPNSubjectcn'. This could indicate that there is VPN certificate sharing occurring. Certificate sharing or other authentication token sharing can make it difficult to identify who's done what. This can complicate taking next steps in the event of a compromise.
conditions_text: when the event context is Local to Local, Remote to Local
when an event matches any of the following UBA : BB : VPN Mapping (logic)
when any of VPN Subject CN (custom) are contained in any of UBA : VPN Subject_CN - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
when any of VPN Subject CN (custom) is the key and any of Username is the value in any of UBA : VPN Subject_CN and UserName - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY UBA : Subject_CN and Username Map Update on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event context is Local to Local, Remote to Local
AND when an event matches any of the following UBA : BB : VPN Mapping (logic)
AND when any of VPN Subject CN (custom) are contained in any of UBA : VPN Subject_CN - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
AND when any of VPN Subject CN (custom) is the key and any of Username is the value in any of UBA : VPN Subject_CN and UserName - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Add the "VPN Subject CN" of the event or flow payload to the Reference Set "UBA : VPN Subject_CN"
Add "userName" to Reference Map: "UBA : VPN Subject_CN and UserName" with key "VPN Subject CN"
